item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with the above that the clinical tone flounders at times. However, I actually really enjoy the backstory. While I was bored somewhat by the initial business with the clans and all that, the story of Eiko and her brother was interesting and, in the end, wholesome. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with the person whose account was deleted: Although I enjoy this article for its connection to real-world events and quality in respect to its narrative, it is not well-suited to be an article in the main SCP series. It needs to be formatted in a more clinical manner, with a more balanced research-to-narrative ratio in relation to content to qualify as a Special Containment Procedures document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite enjoy this one actually. It's a neat little read with some educational bits to it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote here. I can’t get behind how non-clinical the majority of the tone is; the addendum is straight up tale-esque. The item is interesting, cultural (the kanji are a nice touch), and educational and I appreciate those parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is such a tricky one to vote on. It's quite good, I think, but not quite as good as the story that it's based on. It's like a cover of a song that uses different instruments to hit most of the same notes, but something gets lost in translation. +-0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
image source: public domain from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Obake_karuta

item inspiration: The Lenentine Cards, archived here.

like, rate, and subscribe
